title: Review: pre-merge codebase audit
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — two P0 correctness bugs (gcp-sm URL injection, pkcs11 non-atomic put), one efficiency trap that will generate rate-limit reports (bitwarden N+2 calls), and a testing gap in get_blocking. The core abstraction (SecretValue, SecretUri, the JSON scanner, the construction-only from_uri contract) is solid and well-defended. Risk is concentrated in the backends that haven't been integration-tested against live services.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) bitwarden Client SDK thread-safety not verified against SDK source — if Client is not Sync the OnceCell pattern is UB; (2) azure-kv ChainedCredential error handling not verified against actual SDK behavior; (3) doppler and gcp-sm REST API behavior on edge-case HTTP status codes not tested. Would raise confidence if: integration tests for bitwarden and azure-kv pass against live services, and bitwarden SDK source confirms Client: Sync.
